key_insight: Any FEM-like generalized multiscale space with fixed local support and fixed coefficient visibility cannot achieve uniformly vanishing error for all bounded-contrast rough coefficients; its worst-case error stays O(1).
engineering_relevance: This shows that truly scalable multiscale FEM surrogates must relax locality or increase coefficient information as the mesh is refined to retain reliable accuracy.
method: Defines scalability via bounded overlap, fixed support radius, and fixed coefficient-information radius.
Proves an order-one lower bound for deterministic local space constructions in the full measurable coefficient class.
Uses periodic coefficient counterexamples, local patch indistinguishability, and corrector-field approximation arguments.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a theoretical worst-case L2-to-energy Galerkin error lower bound that remains positive and independent of H, established with a finite family of smooth periodic coefficients and smooth right-hand sides.
watch_item: The result is a worst-case impossibility theorem, so practical performance may still be good on restricted coefficient families or with adaptive/nonlocal enrichment.
paper_title: An Order-One Lower Bound on the Error of Scalable Generalized Multiscale Finite Element Space Constructions
